subject: Human Resources
title: OSHA and Workplace Safety
description: A 60-second lesson on OSHA and Workplace Safety.

hook.text: Before OSHA, 14,000 Americans died at work every year. By 2022, that number dropped to about 5,000. Regulation saves lives.

buildup.text: The Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 created OSHA—requiring employers to provide safe workplaces.

discovery.text: OSHA sets and enforces standards for hazards—from chemical exposure to fall protection to ergonomic risks.

twist.text: Many employers see OSHA as bureaucratic. But every standard exists because someone was hurt or killed without it.

climax.text: Smart companies go beyond compliance—building safety cultures where workers report hazards before they cause injuries.

punchline.text: Every safety regulation has a story. Most of them are tragic.

quiz.question: What did OSHA achieve since its creation?
options: Reduced annual workplace deaths from 14,000 to about 5,000
